title: The "Labor-to-Value" Foundation
description: Disconnect from abstract numbers and ground yourself in the reality of money, value, and basic budgeting.

daily_tasks.title: The Needs, Wants, and the 50/30/20 Rule

challenge.title: The Coffee vs. Compound Slider
challenge.description: Adjust a slider to see how saving daily small amounts (like $5) compounds over 10 years at 4% interest.

task.title: Audit Your Last 3 Purchases
task.description: Write down the last 3 things you bought and categorize them strictly into Needs or Wants.

quiz_questions.question: What does the "50" in the 50/30/20 rule stand for?
What does the "20" stand for in the 50/30/20 rule?
Groceries and utilities fall under which category?
Dining out at a fancy restaurant is considered a:
If you earn $1000, how much should ideally go to Wants?
True or False: Savings should only happen if you have money left over at the end of the month.
Which of the following is a Need?
What is "compound interest"?
options: Earning interest on your initial savings and the accumulated interest

quiz_questions.question: Why is the 50/30/20 rule just a guideline?
options: Because it only works for rich people
Because everyone's living situation and income varies

quiz_questions.question: If you receive $100 for your birthday, how much should go into savings under this rule?

daily_tasks.title: Inflation and The True Cost of Things

challenge.title: Time is Money
challenge.description: Input a desired luxury item (e.g., $150 shoes) and your hourly wage/allowance. The app calculates exactly how many hours of work it takes to afford it.

task.title: Find the Shrinkflation
task.description: Find one item in your house or at the supermarket that has stayed the same price but gotten smaller in size over the years.

quiz_questions.question: What is inflation?
options: The general increase in prices over time

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Inflation decreases your purchasing power.
If inflation is 3%, what happens to a $100 bill hidden under your mattress after one year?
options: It buys 3% less than it used to

quiz_questions.question: What is the "True Cost" of an item?
options: The amount of time and labor required to earn enough to buy it

quiz_questions.question: Which group is most negatively affected by high inflation?
What is shrinkflation?
options: Products reducing in size or quantity while pricing remains the same

quiz_questions.question: If you earn $10/hour, how many hours must you work to buy a $50 game?
How can you protect your money from inflation?
options: By investing it in assets that grow faster than the inflation rate

quiz_questions.question: When the price of basic necessities goes up, which category of the 50/30/20 rule is directly impacted?
True or False: Earning 1% interest at the bank means you are beating 3% inflation.

challenge.title: The Rainy Day Scenario
challenge.description: You are presented with 3 simulated emergencies (e.g. broken phone, medical bill). Drag and drop funds from your "Checking" to cover it, showing the stress of being unprepared.

task.title: Start Your Emergency Fund
task.description: Set up an automatic transfer or physically set aside $5 into an "Emergency Only" fund today.

quiz_questions.question: What is an emergency fund?
options: Savings set aside strictly for unexpected, urgent expenses

quiz_questions.question: Which of the following is a valid use of an emergency fund?
Is a new video game release an emergency?
How many months of living expenses should a typical adult aim to save in an emergency fund?
True or False: Teenagers don't need emergency funds because their parents pay for everything.
options: False, teens have unexpected expenses too

quiz_questions.question: Where should you keep an emergency fund?
options: In a highly accessible, liquid bank account

quiz_questions.question: What happens if you use a credit card for an emergency instead of cash reserves?
options: You risk falling into high-interest debt

quiz_questions.question: After using money from your emergency fund, what is the next step?
options: Replenish it as soon as possible

quiz_questions.question: Which of these is NOT an emergency?
Why do people often fail to build an emergency fund?

challenge.title: The 24-Hour Rule
challenge.description: You are presented with 3 impulse buy scenarios (e.g., an online flash sale). You must choose to "Wait 24 Hours," observing how the "need" to buy drops the next day.

task.title: Delay a Want Purchase
task.description: Identify one "Want" you planned to buy this week and delay the purchase by at least 48 hours.

quiz_questions.question: Why do companies use limited-time "Flash Sales"?
options: To trigger FOMO and bypass logical thinking

quiz_questions.question: What is the "24-Hour Rule"?
options: Waiting 24 hours before making a non-essential purchase to avoid impulse buying

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Social media algorithms are designed to show you products you are likely to impulse buy.
Is upgrading a functional phone to the newest model a Need or a Want?
How can you distinguish a true Need from a Want?
options: If you really want it, it's a Need
A Need is required for survival or basic functioning like work/school
If it's on sale, it's a Need
If your friends have it, it's a Need

quiz_questions.question: What is "lifestyle inflation"?
options: Spending more on Wants just because your income has increased

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Buying branded sneakers instead of regular running shoes turns a Need into a Want.
Which emotion is most likely to cause impulse spending?
What is a good strategy to avoid online impulse buys?
options: Remove saved credit card details from shopping apps
Follow more brands on social media

quiz_questions.question: If you delay a purchase by 24 hours and realize you don't actually care about it anymore, what was it?

challenge.title: Fix the Bad Goal
challenge.description: You are given a vague goal ("I want to be rich") and must select the correct components to turn it into a SMART goal.

task.title: Write Your SMART Goal
task.description: Write down one SMART financial goal for the next 30 days and put it somewhere visible.

quiz_questions.question: What does the "S" in SMART stand for?
Why is "I want to save money" NOT a SMART goal?
options: It is not specific or measurable

quiz_questions.question: What makes a goal "Time-bound"?
options: Having a strict deadline or target date

quiz_questions.question: What does the "A" in SMART stand for?
If you earn $100 a month, why is "Save $2000 in two months" a bad goal?
True or False: Writing down a goal increases your chances of achieving it.
What makes a goal "Measurable"?
options: Including exact numbers or dollar amounts so you can track progress

quiz_questions.question: What does "Relevant" mean in goal setting?
options: The goal matters to your personal life plan or values

quiz_questions.question: Which of the following is a SMART goal?
options: I will save $20 a week for 5 weeks to buy a $100 concert ticket

quiz_questions.question: Once you reach a short-term financial goal, what should you do?
options: Celebrate and set a new goal

challenge.title: The Missing Money
challenge.description: Given a mock part-time payslip showing a gross pay of $500 and a net pay of $400, correctly identify the missing $100 as the mandatory CPF contribution.

task.title: Analyze a Receipt
task.description: Look at your last restaurant receipt and calculate how much the GST and Service Charge added to the base price of the food.

quiz_questions.question: What is Gross Pay?
options: The total amount of money earned before any deductions

quiz_questions.question: What is Net Pay?
options: The actual "take-home" pay after taxes and deductions

quiz_questions.question: In Singapore, what is CPF?
options: Central Provident Fund, a mandatory social security savings scheme

quiz_questions.question: True or False: The number on your employment contract is usually your Net Pay.
What does the employer's contribution to CPF mean?
options: The employer takes money from you
The employer pays extra money directly into your CPF account on top of your wage

quiz_questions.question: When eating at a restaurant in Singapore, what is typically added to your bill?
Why is it important to check your payslip?
options: To ensure you are paid correctly for the hours worked and deductions are accurate

quiz_questions.question: If a menu item says "$10++", what does the "++" mean?
True or False: Part-time student workers are exempt from CPF contributions if they earn above a certain threshold.
options: False, all Singaporean/PR employees earning >$500/month must contribute

quiz_questions.question: What is GST?
options: Goods and Services Tax, a tax on domestic consumption

challenge.title: The Snowball Effect
challenge.description: Interactive calculator showing the difference between putting $1000 in a piggy bank (0%) vs. a high-yield savings account (4%) over 10 years.

task.title: Check Your Interest Rate
task.description: Log in to your bank account (or ask your parents about yours) and find out the current interest rate you are earning.

quiz_questions.question: Why do banks pay you interest on your savings?
options: Because they use your deposited money to issue loans to others

quiz_questions.question: What is "principal" in banking?
options: The original sum of money invested or saved

quiz_questions.question: What is compound interest?
options: Interest calculated on the initial principal AND also on the accumulated interest of previous periods
Interest that only applies to loans

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Money kept in a piggy bank loses value over time due to inflation.
If you have $100 in an account paying 5% annual interest, how much will you have after one year?
What is the power of compounding often compared to?
options: A snowball rolling down a hill, getting bigger as it goes

quiz_questions.question: Which is a better factor for growing wealth through compounding?
options: Starting to save as early as possible

quiz_questions.question: What does "p.a." mean when looking at interest rates (e.g., 2% p.a.)?
What happens if you frequently withdraw money from a savings account?
options: You earn less compound interest over time

quiz_questions.question: True or False: All bank accounts offer the exact same interest rate.

title: The Digital Trap
description: Address modern financial pitfalls: E-payments, Buy-Now-Pay-Later (BNPL), and subscription creep.

challenge.title: Audit the Cart
challenge.description: A mock checkout page is shown with various payment options (Full price vs BNPL vs Credit). Select the mathematically cheapest option taking into account hidden processing fees.

task.title: Cancel an Unused Subscription
task.description: Check your recurring subscriptions. Cancel at least one free trial or unused service today.

quiz_questions.question: What does BNPL stand for?
How does BNPL make items seem more affordable?
options: It splits the total cost into smaller, delayed payments

quiz_questions.question: What is the biggest risk of using multiple BNPL services at once?
options: Losing track of payment dates and owing late fees on multiple platforms

quiz_questions.question: True or False: BNPL services never charge fees.
options: False, they often charge late payment fees

quiz_questions.question: What happens if you miss a BNPL payment?
options: You are penalized with a late fee and it can affect your credit score

quiz_questions.question: Why do retailers love offering BNPL?
options: It encourages consumers to spend more money than they normally would

quiz_questions.question: Which is a safer way to buy a $300 item?
options: Save up $300 first, then pay in full
Use a credit card you can't pay off

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Using BNPL is exactly the same as using a debit card.
If you buy a $100 shoe using "Pay in 4" BNPL (4 payments of $25), but miss the last payment and get a $15 late fee, what is the true cost?
Does BNPL feel like spending "real" money?
options: No, which is why it's dangerous

challenge.title: Conversion Calculator
challenge.description: Calculate the real SGD value of a "Special Loot Box Bundle" priced at 4,500 in-game gems.

task.title: Remove Stored Payment Methods
task.description: Go into your phone settings and turn off "In-App Purchases" or remove your stored credit card from an app store.

quiz_questions.question: Why do free-to-play games use "gems" or "crystals" instead of dollars?
options: To disconnect the player from the feeling of spending real money

quiz_questions.question: What is a "dark pattern" in app design?
options: A user interface designed to trick users into doing things, like buying unwanted items

quiz_questions.question: True or False: A $1 in-game purchase is harmless.
options: False, they add up quickly and build spending habits

quiz_questions.question: What is a "Loot Box"?
options: A virtual item that contains randomized rewards, similar to gambling

quiz_questions.question: If 100 gems cost $2, how much real money is a 500 gem skin?
How do apps create a "Fear Of Missing Out" (FOMO)?
What is "subscription creep"?
options: Gradually subscribing to multiple small services until the total monthly cost becomes unmanageable

quiz_questions.question: True or False: You can always get a refund for accidental in-app purchases.
Which is an example of Gamification in spending?
options: Earning 'VIP points' for spending money on a shopping app

quiz_questions.question: The best way to prevent accidental in-app spending is to:
options: Unlink your credit card from the app store

challenge.title: Card Match
challenge.description: Given scenarios (e.g., "Buying a $5 coffee", "Booking a $1000 flight online"), choose whether debit or credit is safer/better, balancing fraud protection against overspending risk.

task.title: Know Your Card
task.description: Find out if your current ATM/Bank card is a Debit card, and check what your daily spending limit is set to.

quiz_questions.question: What happens when you pay with a debit card?
options: The money is immediately deducted from your bank account

quiz_questions.question: What happens when you pay with a credit card?
options: The bank pays the merchant, and you owe the bank that amount

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Credit cards charge interest on your purchases from day one.
options: False, interest is only charged if you don't pay the full balance by the due date

quiz_questions.question: Which card offers better protection against fraud if your details are stolen online?
options: Credit cards, because it is the bank's money on the line initially

quiz_questions.question: Why are credit cards dangerous for undisciplined spenders?
options: They allow you to spend money you do not actually have

quiz_questions.question: What is a credit limit?
options: The maximum amount the bank allows you to borrow on the card

quiz_questions.question: What happens if you only pay the "Minimum Payment" on your credit card bill?
options: You will be charged extremely high interest on the remaining balance

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Debit cards can cause you to fall into massive debt.
options: False, you can generally only spend what you have

quiz_questions.question: Why do credit cards offer points, miles, or cashback?
options: To incentivize you to spend more money using their card

quiz_questions.question: If you do not have a steady income, which card should you use?

challenge.title: Sorting Hat of Debt
challenge.description: Categorize 5 types of loans (e.g., Student Loan, Mortgage, Credit Card Debt for a PS5) into 'Good' or 'Bad' debt buckets.

task.title: Have a Debt Conversation
task.description: Have a conversation with a parent or older sibling about what they consider "Good Debt" and if they have any.

quiz_questions.question: What is "Good Debt"?
options: Borrowing money to invest in something that increases in value or earning potential

quiz_questions.question: Which is generally considered Good Debt?
options: A student loan for higher education

quiz_questions.question: What is "Bad Debt"?
options: Borrowing money for depreciating assets or consumption at high interest rates

quiz_questions.question: Is taking a loan to buy a brand new luxury car usually good or bad debt?
options: Bad debt, because cars rapidly lose value

quiz_questions.question: Why is a housing loan (mortgage) often considered good debt?
options: Because property generally appreciates in value over time

quiz_questions.question: True or False: All debt is completely bad and should be avoided at all costs.
What makes credit card debt the "worst" kind of bad debt?
options: The incredibly high compound interest rates, often 20%+

quiz_questions.question: Taking a personal loan to go on a luxury holiday is an example of:
What is a depreciating asset?
options: Something that loses value over time, like electronics or cars

quiz_questions.question: If a loan helps you increase your future income, it is likely:

challenge.title: The Rogue App
challenge.description: A food delivery app is breached. Decide the safest configuration to have it connected to your bank (e.g., top-up only, low limit, not auto-deducting from main savings).

task.title: Check Your E-Wallet Balance
task.description: Check your PayLah/GrabPay balance. If it is over $100, transfer the excess funds back to your main high-yield bank account.

quiz_questions.question: Why shouldn't you connect your main savings account directly to an E-wallet via auto-deduction?
options: If the E-wallet is compromised, hackers can drain your entire savings

quiz_questions.question: What is the safest way to fund an E-wallet?
options: Manually top up small amounts only when needed

quiz_questions.question: True or False: E-wallets offer the exact same level of security and insurance as traditional bank accounts.
Why do scammers love stealing E-wallet access?
options: Funds can be transferred instantly and are hard to trace

quiz_questions.question: What does setting a "transaction limit" do?
options: It restricts the maximum amount of money that can leave your account in a single day

quiz_questions.question: If you lose your phone, what should your first action be regarding E-wallets?
options: Call your bank to block the linked cards and accounts

quiz_questions.question: True or False: You should use the same password for your E-wallet as your social media accounts.
What is biometrics?
options: Using FaceID or fingerprint scanning to authorize payments

quiz_questions.question: Why is leaving a large balance (e.g., $1000) in an E-wallet a bad idea financially?
options: It earns 0% interest and is exposed to security risks

quiz_questions.question: If an app asks "Remember this card for future purchases?", the safest answer is:

challenge.title: Delivery vs Dabao
challenge.description: Compare the exact same fast-food meal price on a delivery app vs. the physical store, revealing the hidden 30-40% markup on the menu items.

task.title: Walk and Save
task.description: Instead of ordering food delivery today, walk to a nearby hawker centre or buy food directly from the store. Calculate exactly how much money you saved.

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Restaurants charge the exact same price for food on delivery apps as they do in the physical store.
options: False, menu prices are often marked up by 20-30%

quiz_questions.question: What is a "Platform Fee"?
options: An extra charge kept by the app company for maintaining the service

quiz_questions.question: Why do restaurants increase their prices on delivery apps?
options: Because the apps take a large commission (up to 30%) from every order

quiz_questions.question: If a meal costs $10 in-store, but $13 on the app + $3 delivery fee + $1 platform fee, what is the total "Cost of Convenience"?
What is "Surge Pricing" in ride-hailing apps?
options: Prices automatically increasing during high demand or bad weather

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Getting a $0 Delivery Fee promotion means you are paying the same as eating in the store.
options: False, the food items are still marked up

quiz_questions.question: How does using convenience apps frequently affect your budget?
options: It causes rapid "lifestyle inflation" and drains your savings

quiz_questions.question: What is the most financially sensible way to use food delivery apps?
options: As an occasional treat, not a daily habit

quiz_questions.question: Which is a hidden cost of convenience?
options: Missing out on the physical exercise of walking to buy food

quiz_questions.question: If you order delivery 3 times a week and pay an extra $8 each time, how much are you wasting in a month?

challenge.title: Spot the Weak Password
challenge.description: Select the strongest password out of 4 options and explain why length is more important than complexity.

task.title: Enable 2FA
task.description: Turn on 2FA (Two-Factor Authentication) for your primary email account and your most-used social media account.

quiz_questions.question: What is the biggest danger of using the same password for everything?
options: If one website is hacked, the hackers have the password to all your other accounts

quiz_questions.question: What does 2FA stand for?
How does 2FA protect you?
options: It requires a second piece of evidence (like a phone OTP) in addition to your password

quiz_questions.question: What is a Password Manager?
options: A secure app that generates and remembers unique passwords for every site

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Adding an exclamation mark to the end of a dictionary word (e.g., Password123!) makes it highly secure.
What is stronger: A short complex password (d$4!K) or a long passphrase (correct-horse-battery-staple)?
options: A long passphrase because length exponentially increases cracking time

quiz_questions.question: If your email account is hacked, why is it extremely dangerous?
options: Hackers can use it to reset the passwords for all your other accounts, including banking

quiz_questions.question: True or False: You should write your banking password on a sticky note attached to your laptop.
What is an Authenticator App (e.g., Google Authenticator)?
options: An app that generates temporary 2FA codes that change every 30 seconds

quiz_questions.question: Which is considered less secure for 2FA: SMS/Text Messages or an Authenticator App?
options: SMS, because SIM cards can be swapped/hijacked

title: The Scam Dojo
description: Transition from theory to high-stress, gamified simulation to build resistance against modern scams.

challenge.title: The Fake HR Recruiter
challenge.description: A simulated WhatsApp chat with a "recruiter". Tap the 3 red flags (unprofessional number, request for upfront deposit, encrypted app redirect).

task.title: Clean Your Inbox
task.description: Review your SMS or Email inbox. Identify and delete one suspicious/phishing message.

quiz_questions.question: What emotion do scammers try to trigger first?
Will a legitimate government agency ask for your bank password over the phone?
What does it mean if a message says "Act now or your account will be suspended"?
options: It is likely a scam attempting to create urgency
Your account is really in danger

quiz_questions.question: Which of these links is the safest to click?
options: None, you should manually type the official URL into your browser

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Scammers can spoof Caller ID to make it look like the police are calling.
What is a "Safe Account" impersonation scam?
options: A real service from your bank
A scammer pretending to be police telling you to move funds to a 'safe account' they control

quiz_questions.question: If your "bank" texts you a link to verify a transaction, what should you do?
options: Ignore the link and open the official banking app directly

quiz_questions.question: How can you verify official Singapore government links?
options: Check that the URL ends in .gov.sg

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Phishing refers only to email scams.
options: False, it happens via SMS, social media, and calls

quiz_questions.question: What is the exact protocol to report a scam in Singapore?
options: Call the anti-scam helpline or report via the ScamShield app

daily_tasks.title: The "Get Rich Quick" Syndicates (MLMs & Fake Jobs)

challenge.title: The High School Reunion
challenge.description: A simulated chat where an old classmate pitches a "business opportunity". Select assertive dialogue options to shut it down.

task.title: Research Task Scams
task.description: Search online for "Task Scam Singapore" and read one news article about how it starts.

quiz_questions.question: In a pyramid scheme, where does the real money come from?
options: Recruiting new members underneath you, not selling products

quiz_questions.question: True or False: The bottom 99% of a pyramid scheme always lose money.
What is an "E-commerce Task Scam"?
options: Being paid small amounts initially to 'like' items, before being asked to pay large deposits for bigger jobs

quiz_questions.question: Why do task scammers actually pay you a small real commission ($10) in the first round?
options: To build psychological trust before asking for your money

quiz_questions.question: If a job offer requires you to pay an "onboarding fee", what is it likely to be?
What phrase is a massive red flag for investment scams?
options: Guaranteed high returns with zero risk
Past performance doesn't guarantee future results

quiz_questions.question: How do "Get Rich Quick" scammers typically contact victims?
options: Via random WhatsApp, Telegram, or Instagram messages

quiz_questions.question: If an old friend suddenly messages you praising a new "financial mentor", what is likely happening?
options: Their account was hacked or they are trapped in a pyramid scheme

quiz_questions.question: Which is a sign of a fake job listing?
options: Extremely high pay for simple tasks like 'data entry via mobile'

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Scammers often use screenshots of fake bank transfers to prove their "business" works.

challenge.title: The Too-Perfect Match
challenge.description: Review a dating app profile and chat logs to spot the red flags (refuses video calls, talks about crypto early, sudden wealth, claims to live overseas).

task.title: Review Your Privacy Settings
task.description: Review the privacy settings on your social media accounts and ensure your friend list and contact info are not public.

quiz_questions.question: What is a "Pig Butchering" scam?
options: Fattening up a victim with trust and small investment returns before stealing a massive sum

quiz_questions.question: How do romance scammers often initiate contact?
options: A "wrong number" text, an Instagram DM, or a dating app match

quiz_questions.question: Why do romance scammers refuse to do live video calls?
options: Because they are using stolen photos of attractive people

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Romance scams only target lonely elderly people.
options: False, young professionals are huge targets for crypto variations

quiz_questions.question: What is the turning point in a romance/pig butchering scam?
options: They ask to meet in person
When the scammer shifts the conversation from romance to a "guaranteed" investment opportunity

quiz_questions.question: If an online friend of 3 months asks you to invest in a new trading platform, what should you do?
How do scammers gain your trust initially?
options: By mirroring your interests, being overly supportive, and talking daily

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Scammers will sometimes help you make a small, real profit of $50 early on.
options: True, it proves the platform is "real" so you invest more

quiz_questions.question: What happens when you try to withdraw your large "earnings" from the scammer's platform?
options: They freeze the account and demand a "20% tax fee" to unlock it

quiz_questions.question: Why is it dangerous to send compromising photos to someone you met online?
options: They can be used for blackmail/extortion
It's against the law to take photos

challenge.title: Real or Fake?
challenge.description: Identify subtle signs of Deepfake videos (unnatural blinking, weird skin textures, audio out of sync, bizarre requests from authority figures).

task.title: Set a Family Safeword
task.description: Agree on a secret "safeword" or question with your immediate family members to verify their identity if they ever call from an unknown number.

quiz_questions.question: What is a Deepfake?
options: AI-generated media that convincingly replaces someone's likeness or voice

quiz_questions.question: How much audio does an AI need to clone someone's voice?
options: Sometimes as little as 3 seconds, easily taken from social media

quiz_questions.question: If "Elon Musk" is doing a live stream offering to double any crypto you send him, what is it?
What is a good way to verify a distress call from a family member on an unknown number?
options: Ask a personal question only they would know, or use your family safeword
Call them back on the same number

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Deepfakes are always easy to spot because the graphics look like a cartoon.
options: False, modern deepfakes are nearly indistinguishable to the naked eye

quiz_questions.question: If you see a news clip on Facebook of a government minister promoting a trading app, what should you assume?
options: It is almost certainly an AI deepfake

quiz_questions.question: What is a visual clue that a video might be a deepfake?
options: Unnatural blinking patterns and blurring around the mouth/teeth

quiz_questions.question: Why do deepfake video scams often require you to act "within the next 10 minutes"?
options: To trigger panic or FOMO so you don't take time to verify the video

quiz_questions.question: How can you verify a shocking video of a celebrity endorsement?
options: Check reputable news sites and the celebrity's official, verified social media channels

quiz_questions.question: True or False: AI voice cloning can be used to bypass voice-recognition security at some banks.

challenge.title: The Withdrawal Trap
challenge.description: You're in a simulation of a fake crypto app. Try to withdraw your $10,000 "profit" — you're hit with "Account frozen. Pay 20% tax to release funds." Identify this as the final stage of the scam.

task.title: Check the MAS Alert List
task.description: Search for the "MAS Investor Alert List" online to see what official government warnings against unregulated platforms look like.

quiz_questions.question: What is the primary lure of a fake investment platform?
options: Promises of guaranteed, unusually high daily returns

quiz_questions.question: When you log into a fake trading app, what are the numbers you see?
options: Completely fake numbers manipulated by the scammer to look like profit

quiz_questions.question: True or False: If an investment app is on the official App Store, it is 100% safe.
options: False, scam apps sometimes slip past reviewers

quiz_questions.question: What is the "Withdrawal Tax" tactic?
options: When you try to withdraw, the platform demands a massive extra fee to release it

quiz_questions.question: If you pay the "Tax" or "Admin Fee" to withdraw your funds, what happens?
options: You lose that money too, and they still won't release your funds

quiz_questions.question: Who regulates legitimate investment platforms in Singapore?
What happens if you invest with a company on the MAS Investor Alert List?
options: You have zero legal protection when they steal your money

quiz_questions.question: Why do scam platforms ask you to deposit via cryptocurrency or wire transfers?
options: Because those methods are irreversible and hard to trace

quiz_questions.question: "Join this exclusive VIP Telegram group for trading signals." What is this commonly?
options: A pump-and-dump scheme or fake investment platform

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Legitimate banks and brokers will never ask you to pay a tax deposit before you can withdraw.

challenge.title: The Shady APK
challenge.description: You see an ad for a 50% discount on cinema tickets but must download a file called Tickets_Discount_v2.apk via WhatsApp. Actively hit "Reject & Block".

task.title: Check Your Phone Security
task.description: Check your phone settings (Android: Security settings) and ensure "Install Unknown Apps" or "Developer Mode" is disabled unless you actively need it.

quiz_questions.question: What does "sideloading" an app mean?
options: Installing an app from an unverified source outside the official App/Play Store

quiz_questions.question: On Android, what file extension is commonly used for sideloaded apps?
How do scammers trick victims into downloading malware?
options: By offering huge discounts on services but forcing the victim to 'book via our special app'

quiz_questions.question: Once installed, what dangerous permission do malware apps often ask for?
Why does the malware want to read your SMS messages?
options: To intercept the One-Time Passwords (OTPs) your bank sends you

quiz_questions.question: True or False: If an app icon looks like a legitimate cleaning company, the app is safe.
What is a sign that your phone might be infected with malware?
options: The battery drains unusually fast, the phone is hot, and apps open by themselves

quiz_questions.question: If a Facebook ad for "Cheap Seafood" sends you a WhatsApp link to download their ordering app, what should you do?
options: Block the number and do not click the link
Click to check if it's real

quiz_questions.question: Why is it dangerous to give an unverified app "Accessibility" permissions?
options: It allows the app to control your screen and tap buttons on your behalf

quiz_questions.question: What is the safest way to download apps?
options: Only use the official Apple App Store or Google Play Store

challenge.title: The Ultimate Gauntlet
challenge.description: A rapid-fire simulation of 3 different scams occurring simultaneously: an urgent call from "Singpost", a WhatsApp from "HR" about a job, and an Instagram DM about crypto. Correctly identify, block, and report all 3.

task.title: Install ScamShield
task.description: Download and install the ScamShield app by the Singapore Government (or help an elderly relative install and configure it on their phone today).

quiz_questions.question: Which combination of emotions do scammers rely on most?
What is the golden rule when receiving a call from "The Police" asking for money/passwords?
options: Hang up immediately, the real police never ask for money/passwords

quiz_questions.question: If something sounds too good to be true, it probably is. This describes which scam type?
What is the purpose of the ScamShield app?
options: To automatically block known scam calls and SMSes in Singapore

quiz_questions.question: True or False: Scammers only target the elderly and technologically illiterate.
options: False, anyone can be caught off-guard or manipulated

quiz_questions.question: Your parcel delivery 'failed'. Click here: www.singp0st-update.com. What is wrong?
options: The URL is fake (singp0st) and it's a phishing attempt
You need to update your address

quiz_questions.question: If your friend messages asking you to buy them Steam gift cards urgently, what should you do?
options: Call them directly on the phone to verify their identity
Send the gift card codes via text

quiz_questions.question: What is the most irreversible form of payment that scammers demand?
Who is responsible for protecting your money from authorized push payment scams?
options: You are; the bank usually cannot recover the funds

quiz_questions.question: What is the best defense against all scams?
options: Pause, verify independently, and never act out of panic
